evals.description: Agent should create a valid hook script with proper structure
evals.prompt: Create a hook that blocks commits containing TODO comments in production code
expected_behaviors: Creates a .sh file with executable permissions
Includes proper shebang line (#!/bin/bash or #!/usr/bin/env bash)
Reads input from environment variables (TOOL_NAME, FILE_PATH, FILE_CONTENT)
Outputs valid JSON with result (accept/reject) and message
Handles edge cases (empty input, missing variables)
unexpected_behaviors: Creates a Python or Node.js hook instead of shell script
No error handling for missing environment variables
Uses hardcoded file paths instead of environment variables

evals.description: Agent should select an appropriate reference template
evals.prompt: Create a hook that prevents API keys from being committed to the repository
expected_behaviors: Reads secret-detector.sh from references/ as starting template
Adapts the template patterns for API key detection
Includes common API key patterns (AWS, GCP, GitHub, etc.)
Tests against sample files to verify detection
unexpected_behaviors: Creates hook from scratch ignoring available templates
Uses wrong template (e.g., build-reminder for a security hook)
